content: Just as the linguistic meaning of 'reflexion' encompasses both physical light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection in human learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate a dual 'reflexion' framework: a fast 'reflection' step that mirrors existing output responses back to the model like light bouncing off a surface, and a deliberate 'reflexion' step that draws on human-style learning reflection to critique inconsistent outputs across perspectives. This dual framework can resolve the limitation of current single-perspective LLM self-reflection that fails to catch hidden logical flaws.
